1→"""Tests for the unified recommendation pipeline.
     2→
     3→Verifies that both REST and agent paths produce display_product payloads
     4→that the mirror's ClothingCanvas can consume.
     5→"""
     6→
     7→import time
     8→from unittest.mock import AsyncMock, MagicMock, patch
     9→
    10→import pytest
    11→
    12→from agent.orchestrator import (
    13→    SessionState,
    14→    _outfits_to_display_payloads,
    15→)
    16→
    17→
    18→# --- _outfits_to_display_payloads ---
    19→
    20→
    21→def _make_outfit(items, outfit_name="Test Outfit"):
    22→    """Helper to build an outfit dict matching the REST pipeline format."""
    23→    return {
    24→        "outfit_name": outfit_name,
    25→        "items": [
    26→            {
    27→                "type": item_type,
    28→                "item": {
    29→                    "title": title,
    30→                    "price": "$50",
    31→                    "image_url": "https://example.com/raw.jpg",
    32→                    "product_id": f"pid-{i}",
    33→                    "link": "https://example.com",
    34→                    "source": "test",
    35→                    **(extra or {}),
    36→                },
    37→            }
    38→            for i, (item_type, title, extra) in enumerate(items)
    39→        ],
    40→    }
    41→
    42→
    43→class TestOutfitsToDisplayPayloads:
    44→    def test_converts_outfits_with_flat_lays(self):
    45→        """Items with flat lay images are included in the payload."""
    46→        outfits = [
    47→            _make_outfit([
    48→                ("top", "Blue Shirt", {"cleaned_image_url": "data:image/png;base64,abc"}),
    49→                ("bottom", "Black Pants", {"flat_image_url": "data:image/png;base64,def"}),
    50→            ])
    51→        ]
    52→        payloads = _outfits_to_display_payloads(outfits)
    53→
    54→        assert len(payloads) == 1
    55→        assert payloads[0]["type"] == "display_product"
    56→        assert payloads[0]["outfit_name"] == "Test Outfit"
    57→        assert len(payloads[0]["items"]) == 2
    58→        assert payloads[0]["items"][0]["title"] == "Blue Shirt"
    59→        assert payloads[0]["items"][0]["type"] == "top"
    60→        assert payloads[0]["items"][1]["title"] == "Black Pants"
    61→
    62→    def test_skips_items_without_flat_lays(self):
    63→        """Items with only raw image_url (no flat lay) are excluded."""
    64→        outfits = [
    65→            _make_outfit([
    66→                ("top", "Has Flat Lay", {"cleaned_image_url": "data:image/png;base64,abc"}),
    67→                ("bottom", "No Flat Lay", {}),  # no cleaned_image_url or flat_image_url
    68→            ])
    69→        ]
    70→        payloads = _outfits_to_display_payloads(outfits)
    71→
    72→        assert len(payloads) == 1
    73→        assert len(payloads[0]["items"]) == 1
    74→        assert payloads[0]["items"][0]["title"] == "Has Flat Lay"
    75→
    76→    def test_empty_when_no_flat_lays(self):
    77→        """Returns empty list when no items have flat lay images."""
    78→        outfits = [
    79→            _make_outfit([
    80→                ("top", "Raw Only", {}),
    81→                ("bottom", "Also Raw", {}),
    82→            ])
    83→        ]
    84→        payloads = _outfits_to_display_payloads(outfits)
    85→        assert payloads == []
    86→
    87→    def test_multiple_outfits(self):
    88→        """Each outfit becomes a separate payload."""
    89→        outfits = [
    90→            _make_outfit(
    91→                [("top", "Shirt A", {"cleaned_image_url": "data:a"})],
    92→                outfit_name="Outfit A",
    93→            ),
    94→            _make_outfit(
    95→                [("bottom", "Pants B", {"flat_image_url": "data:b"})],
    96→                outfit_name="Outfit B",
    97→            ),
    98→        ]
    99→        payloads = _outfits_to_display_payloads(outfits)
   100→        assert len(payloads) == 2
   101→        assert payloads[0]["outfit_name"] == "Outfit A"
   102→        assert payloads[1]["outfit_name"] == "Outfit B"
   103→
   104→    def test_empty_outfits_list(self):
   105→        """Empty input returns empty output."""
   106→        assert _outfits_to_display_payloads([]) == []
   107→
   108→
   109→# --- display_product tool generates flat lays ---
   110→
   111→
   112→class TestDisplayProduct:
   113→    @pytest.mark.asyncio
   114→    async def test_display_product_calls_gemini(self):
   115→        """_display_product calls Gemini and maps flat lay URLs onto items."""
   116→        from agent.tools import _display_product
   117→
   118→        mock_flat_lays = {"pid-1": "data:image/png;base64,flatlay1"}
   119→
   120→        mock_module = MagicMock()
   121→        mock_module.generate_flat_lays_batch = AsyncMock(return_value=mock_flat_lays)
   122→
   123→        with patch.dict("sys.modules", {"services.gemini_flatlay": mock_module}):
   124→            result = await _display_product({
   125→                "items": [
   126→                    {"image_url": "https://img.com/1.jpg", "title": "Test Shirt", "product_id": "pid-1", "type": "top"},
   127→                ],
   128→                "outfit_name": "Test Outfit",
   129→            })
   130→
   131→        assert result["displayed"] == 1
   132→        assert result["items"][0]["cleaned_image_url"] == "data:image/png;base64,flatlay1"
   133→        assert result["items"][0]["flat_image_url"] == "data:image/png;base64,flatlay1"
   134→        assert result["frontend_payload"]["type"] == "display_product"
   135→        assert result["frontend_payload"]["outfit_name"] == "Test Outfit"
   136→
   137→
   138→# --- give_recommendation caching ---
   139→
   140→
   141→class TestGiveRecommendationCache:
   142→    @pytest.mark.asyncio
   143→    async def test_caches_serper_results(self):
   144→        """execute_give_recommendation stores results in serper_cache, second call hits cache."""
   145→        from agent.tools import execute_give_recommendation
   146→        from services.serper_cache import serper_cache
   147→
   148→        session_id = "test-session-cache"
   149→        serper_cache._cache.pop(session_id, None)  # ensure clean state
   150→
   151→        mock_items = [
   152→            {"title": "Shirt", "price": "$30", "image_url": "https://img.com/1.jpg",
   153→             "product_id": "p1", "link": "https://example.com", "source": "Serper",
   154→             "rating": None, "clothing_category": "top"},
   155→        ]
   156→
   157→        with patch("agent.tools.fetch_clothing_batch", new_callable=AsyncMock, return_value=mock_items):
   158→            result1 = await execute_give_recommendation({"brands": ["Nike"], "gender": "mens"}, session_id)
   159→
   160→        # Second call should hit cache (no fetch_clothing_batch call needed)
   161→        with patch("agent.tools.fetch_clothing_batch", new_callable=AsyncMock) as mock_fetch:
   162→            result2 = await execute_give_recommendation({"brands": ["Nike"], "gender": "mens"}, session_id)
   163→            mock_fetch.assert_not_called()
   164→
   165→        assert "Shirt" in result1
   166→        assert "Shirt" in result2
   167→
   168→        # Cleanup
   169→        serper_cache._cache.pop(session_id, None)
   170→
   171→
   172→# --- session_started socket handler ---
   173→
   174→
   175→class TestSessionStartedHandler:
   176→    @pytest.mark.asyncio
   177→    async def test_emits_display_product_not_outfits_ready(self):
   178→        """session_started handler emits tool_result events, not outfits_ready."""
   179→        from main import session_started
   180→
   181→        mock_sio = AsyncMock()
   182→
   183→        outfits = [
   184→            _make_outfit([
   185→                ("top", "Test Top", {"cleaned_image_url": "data:top"}),
   186→                ("bottom", "Test Bottom", {"flat_image_url": "data:bottom"}),
   187→            ], outfit_name="Casual Look"),
   188→        ]
   189→
   190→        mock_result = {
   191→            "status": "success",
   192→            "data": {"outfits": outfits, "greeting": "Hey!", "style_analysis": "Cool"},
   193→        }
   194→
   195→        with patch("main.get_neon_client", new_callable=AsyncMock) as mock_db, \
   196→             patch("main.generate_outfit_recommendations", new_callable=AsyncMock, return_value=mock_result), \
   197→             patch("main.sio", mock_sio):
   198→            mock_db.return_value = AsyncMock()
   199→
   200→            await session_started("sid-123", {"session_id": "sess-1", "user_id": "user-1"})
   201→
   202→        # Should NOT have emitted outfits_ready
   203→        outfits_ready_calls = [
   204→            c for c in mock_sio.emit.call_args_list
   205→            if c.args[0] == "outfits_ready"
   206→        ]
   207→        assert len(outfits_ready_calls) == 0
   208→
   209→        # Should have emitted tool_result with display_product type
   210→        tool_result_calls = [
   211→            c for c in mock_sio.emit.call_args_list
   212→            if c.args[0] == "tool_result"
   213→        ]
   214→        assert len(tool_result_calls) >= 1
   215→        payload = tool_result_calls[0].args[1]
   216→        assert payload["type"] == "display_product"
   217→        assert payload["outfit_name"] == "Casual Look"
   218→        assert len(payload["items"]) == 2

1→"""Tests for conversation history integrity — concurrency, validation, and recovery.
     2→
     3→Covers the asyncio.Lock event serialization, _validate_history() defense-in-depth,
     4→and end-of-message signaling in error paths. These tests prevent the permanent 400
     5→error bug where concurrent events corrupt conversation_history by interleaving
     6→user messages between tool_use and tool_result blocks.
     7→"""
     8→
     9→import asyncio
    10→from types import SimpleNamespace
    11→from unittest.mock import AsyncMock, patch
    12→
    13→import pytest
    14→
    15→from agent.orchestrator import MiraOrchestrator, SessionState
    16→
    17→
    18→# --- Helpers (matching test_error_recovery.py patterns) ---
    19→
    20→
    21→def _make_orchestrator() -> MiraOrchestrator:
    22→    """Create an orchestrator with mocked Socket.io."""
    23→    return MiraOrchestrator(socket_io=AsyncMock())
    24→
    25→
    26→def _make_session(history: list | None = None) -> SessionState:
    27→    """Create a session with optional pre-populated conversation history."""
    28→    session = SessionState(user_id="test-user")
    29→    session.conversation_history = list(history) if history else []
    30→    session.system_prompt = "You are a test assistant."
    31→    return session
    32→
    33→
    34→def _make_tool_use_block(**kwargs):
    35→    """Create a SimpleNamespace mimicking an Anthropic SDK tool_use content block."""
    36→    defaults = {"type": "tool_use", "id": "tu_123", "name": "search_clothing", "input": {}}
    37→    defaults.update(kwargs)
    38→    return SimpleNamespace(**defaults)
    39→
    40→
    41→def _make_text_block(text="Hello"):
    42→    """Create a SimpleNamespace mimicking an Anthropic SDK text content block."""
    43→    return SimpleNamespace(type="text", text=text)
    44→
    45→
    46→def _make_final_message(content_blocks):
    47→    """Create a SimpleNamespace mimicking an Anthropic SDK final message."""
    48→    return SimpleNamespace(content=content_blocks, stop_reason="end_turn")
    49→
    50→
    51→# --- Concurrency tests ---
    52→
    53→
    54→@pytest.mark.asyncio
    55→async def test_concurrent_events_serialized():
    56→    """Two events entering handle_event simultaneously are serialized by the lock.
    57→
    58→    Without the lock, both coroutines would append user messages before either
    59→    calls Claude, resulting in consecutive user messages (corruption). With the
    60→    lock, the second event waits for the first to finish.
    61→    """
    62→    orch = _make_orchestrator()
    63→    session = _make_session()
    64→    orch.sessions["test-user"] = session
    65→
    66→    call_order = []
    67→
    68→    original_call_claude = orch._call_claude
    69→
    70→    async def mock_call_claude(sess, tool_depth=0):
    71→        call_order.append(f"claude_start_{len(sess.conversation_history)}")
    72→        # Simulate Claude taking time (yields control to event loop)
    73→        await asyncio.sleep(0.05)
    74→        # Append an assistant response to keep history valid
    75→        sess.conversation_history.append({
    76→            "role": "assistant",
    77→            "content": [_make_text_block("Response")],
    78→        })
    79→        call_order.append(f"claude_end_{len(sess.conversation_history)}")
    80→
    81→    with patch.object(orch, "_call_claude", side_effect=mock_call_claude):
    82→        with patch.object(orch, "_start_silence_timer"):
    83→            # Fire two events concurrently
    84→            await asyncio.gather(
    85→                orch.handle_event("test-user", {"type": "voice", "transcript": "First"}),
    86→                orch.handle_event("test-user", {"type": "voice", "transcript": "Second"}),
    87→            )
    88→
    89→    # With the lock, events are serialized: first completes fully before second starts
    90→    # History should be: user, assistant, user, assistant (valid alternation)
    91→    assert len(session.conversation_history) == 4
    92→    assert session.conversation_history[0]["role"] == "user"
    93→    assert session.conversation_history[1]["role"] == "assistant"
    94→    assert session.conversation_history[2]["role"] == "user"
    95→    assert session.conversation_history[3]["role"] == "assistant"
    96→
    97→    # Claude calls should NOT overlap — second starts after first ends
    98→    assert call_order[0].startswith("claude_start")
    99→    assert call_order[1].startswith("claude_end")
   100→    assert call_order[2].startswith("claude_start")
   101→    assert call_order[3].startswith("claude_end")
   102→
   103→
   104→@pytest.mark.asyncio
   105→async def test_event_during_tool_execution():
   106→    """Event arriving while tools are executing is queued by the lock.
   107→
   108→    Simulates: event A triggers Claude → tool_use → tool execution (long await).
   109→    Event B arrives during tool execution. Without the lock, B would append a user
   110→    message between assistant tool_use and user tool_result, corrupting history.
   111→    """
   112→    orch = _make_orchestrator()
   113→    session = _make_session()
   114→    orch.sessions["test-user"] = session
   115→
   116→    tool_execution_event = asyncio.Event()
   117→    second_event_started = asyncio.Event()
   118→
   119→    async def slow_call_claude(sess, tool_depth=0):
   120→        # First call: simulate tool execution delay
   121→        if tool_depth == 0 and len(sess.conversation_history) == 1:
   122→            sess.conversation_history.append({
   123→                "role": "assistant",
   124→                "content": [_make_tool_use_block()],
   125→            })
   126→            # Signal that tool execution is "in progress"
   127→            tool_execution_event.set()
   128→            await asyncio.sleep(0.05)  # Simulate tool execution time
   129→            sess.conversation_history.append({
   130→                "role": "user",
   131→                "content": [{"type": "tool_result", "tool_use_id": "tu_123", "content": "{}"}],
   132→            })
   133→            # Second Claude call for tool result processing
   134→            sess.conversation_history.append({
   135→                "role": "assistant",
   136→                "content": [_make_text_block("Here's what I found")],
   137→            })
   138→        else:
   139→            # Subsequent calls
   140→            sess.conversation_history.append({
   141→                "role": "assistant",
   142→                "content": [_make_text_block("Sure thing")],
   143→            })
   144→
   145→    async def delayed_second_event():
   146→        # Wait until tool execution is in progress, then fire second event
   147→        await tool_execution_event.wait()
   148→        second_event_started.set()
   149→        await orch.handle_event("test-user", {"type": "voice", "transcript": "Also show me shoes"})
   150→
   151→    with patch.object(orch, "_call_claude", side_effect=slow_call_claude):
   152→        with patch.object(orch, "_start_silence_timer"):
   153→            await asyncio.gather(
   154→                orch.handle_event("test-user", {"type": "voice", "transcript": "Find me a jacket"}),
   155→                delayed_second_event(),
   156→            )
   157→
   158→    # Verify: no consecutive user messages, no orphaned tool_use
   159→    for i in range(len(session.conversation_history) - 1):
   160→        curr_role = session.conversation_history[i]["role"]
   161→        next_role = session.conversation_history[i + 1]["role"]
   162→        # user → user is never valid
   163→        assert not (curr_role == "user" and next_role == "user"), (
   164→            f"Consecutive user messages at index {i} and {i+1}"
   165→        )
   166→
   167→
   168→# --- History validation tests ---
   169→
   170→
   171→@pytest.mark.asyncio
   172→async def test_validate_history_orphaned_tool_use():
   173→    """History with tool_use but no following tool_result is truncated."""
   174→    orch = _make_orchestrator()
   175→    session = _make_session([
   176→        {"role": "user", "content": "Hello"},
   177→        {"role": "assistant", "content": [_make_text_block("Hi!")]},
   178→        {"role": "user", "content": "Find me a jacket"},
   179→        {"role": "assistant", "content": [_make_tool_use_block()]},
   180→        # Missing tool_result — corrupted!
   181→    ])
   182→
   183→    orch._validate_history(session)
   184→
   185→    # Should truncate to before the orphaned tool_use assistant message
   186→    assert len(session.conversation_history) == 3
   187→    assert session.conversation_history[-1]["role"] == "user"
   188→    assert session.conversation_history[-1]["content"] == "Find me a jacket"
   189→
   190→
   191→@pytest.mark.asyncio
   192→async def test_validate_history_consecutive_user_messages():
   193→    """Two consecutive user messages are truncated at the second one."""
   194→    orch = _make_orchestrator()
   195→    session = _make_session([
   196→        {"role": "user", "content": "Hello"},
   197→        {"role": "assistant", "content": [_make_text_block("Hi!")]},
   198→        {"role": "user", "content": "First question"},
   199→        {"role": "user", "content": "Second question"},  # corruption
   200→    ])
   201→
   202→    orch._validate_history(session)
   203→
   204→    # Should truncate before the second consecutive user message
   205→    assert len(session.conversation_history) == 3
   206→    assert session.conversation_history[-1]["content"] == "First question"
   207→
   208→
   209→@pytest.mark.asyncio
   210→async def test_validate_history_clean_passthrough():
   211→    """Valid history passes through unchanged."""
   212→    orch = _make_orchestrator()
   213→    valid_history = [
   214→        {"role": "user", "content": "Hello"},
   215→        {"role": "assistant", "content": [_make_text_block("Hi!")]},
   216→        {"role": "user", "content": "Find me shoes"},
   217→        {"role": "assistant", "content": [_make_tool_use_block(id="tu_456")]},
   218→        {"role": "user", "content": [{"type": "tool_result", "tool_use_id": "tu_456", "content": "{}"}]},
   219→        {"role": "assistant", "content": [_make_text_block("Here are some shoes")]},
   220→        {"role": "user", "content": "Thanks!"},
   221→    ]
   222→    session = _make_session(valid_history)
   223→
   224→    orch._validate_history(session)
   225→
   226→    assert len(session.conversation_history) == 7
   227→
   228→
   229→@pytest.mark.asyncio
   230→async def test_validate_history_deep_corruption():
   231→    """Corruption at messages[2] (matching the real error) is repaired.
   232→
   233→    This reproduces the exact scenario from the logs: a user message inserted
   234→    between an assistant tool_use and its expected tool_result.
   235→    """
   236→    orch = _make_orchestrator()
   237→    session = _make_session([
   238→        {"role": "user", "content": "Show me jackets"},
   239→        {"role": "assistant", "content": [_make_tool_use_block(id="tu_real")]},
   240→        # THIS IS THE CORRUPTION: a regular user message instead of tool_result
   241→        {"role": "user", "content": "Also I like blue"},
   242→        # The tool_result that should have been at index 2
   243→        {"role": "user", "content": [{"type": "tool_result", "tool_use_id": "tu_real", "content": "{}"}]},
   244→    ])
   245→
   246→    orch._validate_history(session)
   247→
   248→    # Should truncate at the corruption point — the assistant tool_use at index 1
   249→    # has no valid tool_result following it
   250→    assert len(session.conversation_history) == 1
   251→    assert session.conversation_history[0]["content"] == "Show me jackets"
   252→
   253→
   254→# --- Silence timer + lock interaction ---
   255→
   256→
   257→@pytest.mark.asyncio
   258→async def test_is_processing_prevents_silence_during_lock():
   259→    """Silence timer correctly skips when processing is locked.
   260→
   261→    The silence callback checks `is_processing` before calling handle_event.
   262→    When the lock is held, is_processing is True, so silence is suppressed.
   263→    """
   264→    orch = _make_orchestrator()
   265→    session = _make_session()
   266→    orch.sessions["test-user"] = session
   267→
   268→    # Simulate: lock is held, is_processing=True
   269→    session.is_processing = True
   270→
   271→    # Directly test the silence callback logic
   272→    silence_event_fired = False
   273→
   274→    original_handle_event = orch.handle_event
   275→
   276→    async def tracking_handle_event(user_id, event):
   277→        nonlocal silence_event_fired
   278→        if event.get("type") == "silence":
   279→            silence_event_fired = True
   280→        await original_handle_event(user_id, event)
   281→
   282→    with patch.object(orch, "handle_event", side_effect=tracking_handle_event):
   283→        # The silence callback checks is_processing — since it's True, it should not fire
   284→        if session.is_active and not session.is_processing:
   285→            await orch.handle_event("test-user", {"type": "silence", "duration_seconds": 5})
   286→
   287→    assert not silence_event_fired
   288→
   289→
   290→# --- End-of-message signaling ---
   291→
   292→
   293→@pytest.mark.asyncio
   294→async def test_fallback_sends_end_of_message():
   295→    """Outer catch in handle_event sends both fallback text AND end_of_message signal."""
   296→    orch = _make_orchestrator()
   297→    session = _make_session()
   298→    orch.sessions["test-user"] = session
   299→
   300→    with patch.object(orch, "_call_claude", side_effect=Exception("Unexpected crash")):
   301→        with patch.object(orch, "_stream_text", new_callable=AsyncMock) as mock_stream:
   302→            with patch.object(orch, "_start_silence_timer"):
   303→                await orch.handle_event("test-user", {"type": "voice", "transcript": "Hello"})
   304→
   305→    # Should have been called twice: once with fallback text, once with end_of_message
   306→    assert mock_stream.call_count == 2
   307→    fallback_call = mock_stream.call_args_list[0]
   308→    assert "glitched" in fallback_call.args[1]
   309→    eom_call = mock_stream.call_args_list[1]
   310→    assert eom_call.args[1] == ""
   311→    assert eom_call.kwargs.get("end_of_message") is True
   312→
   313→
   314→# --- Error recovery after tool chain ---
   315→
   316→
   317→@pytest.mark.asyncio
   318→async def test_error_recovery_after_tool_chain():
   319→    """API failure during depth-2 tool recursion recovers cleanly.
   320→
   321→    Simulates: user → Claude (tool_use) → tool_result → Claude (fails).
   322→    After recovery, history should be valid for the next turn.
   323→    """
   324→    orch = _make_orchestrator()
   325→
   326→    # Build history as if we're mid-tool-chain
   327→    session = _make_session([
   328→        {"role": "user", "content": "Find me a jacket"},
   329→        {"role": "assistant", "content": [_make_tool_use_block(id="tu_first")]},
   330→        {"role": "user", "content": [{"type": "tool_result", "tool_use_id": "tu_first", "content": '{"items": []}'}]},
   331→    ])
   332→
   333→    # Second Claude call (processing tool results) fails
   334→    with patch.object(orch.client.messages, "stream", side_effect=Exception("API overloaded")):
   335→        await orch._call_claude(session, tool_depth=1)
   336→
   337→    # Error handler should pop the tool_result user message and orphaned tool_use assistant
   338→    # Validate that history is now clean for the next turn
   339→    orch._validate_history(session)
   340→
   341→    # History should still be valid after recovery + validation
   342→    for i in range(len(session.conversation_history) - 1):
   343→        curr_role = session.conversation_history[i]["role"]
   344→        next_role = session.conversation_history[i + 1]["role"]
   345→        assert curr_role != next_role or curr_role == "user", (
   346→            f"Invalid role sequence at index {i}: {curr_role} → {next_role}"
   347→        )
   348→

1→"""Tests for Socket.io scrape progress events."""
     2→
     3→import pytest
     4→from unittest.mock import AsyncMock
     5→from scraper.socket_events import emit_scrape_progress, emit_scrape_complete
     6→
     7→
     8→@pytest.mark.asyncio
     9→async def test_emit_scrape_progress():
    10→    """emit_scrape_progress emits scrape_progress event to user's room."""
    11→    sio = AsyncMock()
    12→    await emit_scrape_progress(
    13→        sio,
    14→        user_id="user-123",
    15→        purchases_count=5,
    16→        brands_found=["Nike", "Zara"],
    17→        phase="fast",
    18→    )
    19→    sio.emit.assert_called_once_with(
    20→        "scrape_progress",
    21→        {
    22→            "user_id": "user-123",
    23→            "purchases_count": 5,
    24→            "brands_found": ["Nike", "Zara"],
    25→            "phase": "fast",
    26→        },
    27→        room="user-123",
    28→    )
    29→
    30→
    31→@pytest.mark.asyncio
    32→async def test_emit_scrape_complete():
    33→    """emit_scrape_complete emits scrape_complete with profile."""
    34→    sio = AsyncMock()
    35→    profile = {"brands": ["Nike"], "price_range": {"min": 50, "max": 200, "avg": 100}}
    36→    await emit_scrape_complete(sio, user_id="user-123", profile=profile)
    37→    sio.emit.assert_called_once()
    38→    call_args = sio.emit.call_args
    39→    assert call_args[0][0] == "scrape_complete"
    40→    assert call_args[0][1]["profile"] == profile
    41→

1→"""Tests for queue: join, position calculation, and status transitions."""
     2→
     3→import os
     4→import uuid
     5→
     6→import pytest
     7→import pytest_asyncio
     8→from dotenv import load_dotenv
     9→
    10→load_dotenv(os.path.join(os.path.dirname(__file__), "..", ".env"))
    11→
    12→from tests.test_database import NeonSQL, DATABASE_URL
    13→
    14→
    15→@pytest_asyncio.fixture(scope="session")
    16→async def db():
    17→    return NeonSQL(DATABASE_URL)
    18→
    19→
    20→@pytest_asyncio.fixture
    21→async def queue_user(db):
    22→    """Create a test user for queue tests, clean up after."""
    23→    email = f"queue_{uuid.uuid4().hex[:8]}@mirrorless.test"
    24→    rows = await db.execute(
    25→        "INSERT INTO users (name, email) VALUES ($1, $2) RETURNING id",
    26→        ["Queue Tester", email],
    27→    )
    28→    user_id = rows[0]["id"]
    29→    yield user_id
    30→    await db.execute("DELETE FROM queue WHERE user_id = $1::uuid", [user_id])
    31→    await db.execute("DELETE FROM users WHERE id = $1::uuid", [user_id])
    32→
    33→

1→"""Tests for Neon Postgres database schema and CRUD operations.
     2→
     3→Uses Neon's serverless HTTP API (port 443) since port 5432 may be blocked
     4→in some environments. The HTTP API uses the Neon-Connection-String header.
     5→"""
     6→
     7→import os
     8→import uuid
     9→
    10→import httpx
    11→import pytest
    12→import pytest_asyncio
    13→from dotenv import load_dotenv

    16→

    18→

    20→    "clothing_items",
    21→    "purchases",
    22→    "queue",
    23→    "session_outfits",
    24→    "sessions",
    25→    "style_profiles",
    26→    "users",
    27→]
    28→
    29→
    30→class NeonSQL:
    31→    """Test helper: execute SQL via Neon's serverless HTTP endpoint."""
    32→
    33→    def __init__(self, connection_string: str):
    34→        self.connection_string = connection_string
    35→        from urllib.parse import urlparse
    36→
    37→        parsed = urlparse(connection_string)
    38→        self.api_url = f"https://{parsed.hostname}/sql"
    39→
    40→    async def execute(self, query: str, params: list | None = None) -> list[dict]:
    41→        async with httpx.AsyncClient(timeout=30) as client:
    42→            resp = await client.post(
    43→                self.api_url,
    44→                json={"query": query, "params": params or []},
    45→                headers={
    46→                    "Content-Type": "application/json",
    47→                    "Neon-Connection-String": self.connection_string,
    48→                },
    49→            )
    50→            data = resp.json()
    51→            if resp.status_code != 200:
    52→                error_msg = data.get("message", resp.text)
    53→                raise Exception(f"SQL error: {error_msg}")
    54→            return data.get("rows", [])
    55→
    56→    async def fetchval(self, query: str, params: list | None = None):
    57→        rows = await self.execute(query, params)
    58→        if rows:
    59→            return list(rows[0].values())[0]
    60→        return None
    61→
    62→    async def fetchrow(self, query: str, params: list | None = None) -> dict | None:
    63→        rows = await self.execute(query, params)
    64→        return rows[0] if rows else None
    65→

    81→    yield user_id
    82→    await db.execute("DELETE FROM users WHERE id = $1", [user_id])
    83→
    84→

    89→

    93→    assert result == 1

Perfect! Now I have a complete picture. Let me create a comprehensive summary document:

## Orchestrator Test Structure Summary

I've thoroughly analyzed the orchestrator test patterns in `backend/tests/`. Here's what I found:

### **Key Findings**

#### **1. No conftest.py at backend/tests/ level**
- No centralized pytest configuration file
- Each test file is self-contained
- Tests use inline fixture definitions with `@pytest_asyncio.fixture`

#### **2. Test Files Related to Orchestrator/Agent**

| File | Purpose | Key Pattern |
|------|---------|-------------|
| **test_mira.py** | Integration tests for Mira agent, system prompt building, tool execution, live Claude API calls | Direct Anthropic async client usage with mock data |
| **test_error_recovery.py** | Error handling during `_call_claude()` — orphaned tool_use cleanup | AsyncMock Socket.io, SimpleNamespace for SDK blocks |
| **test_history_integrity.py** | Conversation history validation, concurrency serialization, validation logic | asyncio locks, concurrent event simulation |
| **test_unified_pipeline.py** | Outfit display payload conversion, tool execution caching, session started handler | Mocked Gemini, caching logic, display_product format |
| **test_pipeline.py** | Scraping pipeline orchestrator with mocked Gmail service | MagicMock for Gmail service chaining |
| **test_socket_events.py** | Socket.io event emission (scrape progress, completion) | Direct AsyncMock for socket.io emit calls |

#### **3. Orchestrator/Claude Mocking Patterns**

**AsyncMock for Orchestrator:**
```python
from unittest.mock import AsyncMock, MagicMock, patch

orch = MiraOrchestrator(socket_io=AsyncMock())

# Mock Claude client stream
with patch.object(orch.client.messages, "stream", side_effect=Exception("API down")):
    await orch._call_claude(session)
```

**Anthropic SDK Blocks as SimpleNamespace:**
```python
def _make_tool_use_block(**kwargs):
    defaults = {"type": "tool_use", "id": "tu_123", "name": "search_clothing", "input": {}}
    defaults.update(kwargs)
    return SimpleNamespace(**defaults)
```

**Direct Anthropic Client Usage (for integration tests):**
```python
import anthropic

client = anthropic.AsyncAnthropic(
    auth_token=auth_token,
    default_headers={"anthropic-beta": "oauth-2025-04-20"},
    default_query={"beta": "true"},
)

response = await client.messages.create(
    model="claude-haiku-4-5-20251001",
    max_tokens=200,
    system=system_prompt,
    messages=messages,
    tools=TOOL_DEFINITIONS,
)
```

#### **4. SessionState and Conversation History**

**SessionState fixture pattern:**
```python
def _make_session(history: list | None = None) -> SessionState:
    session = SessionState(user_id="test-user")
    session.conversation_history = list(history) if history else []
    session.system_prompt = "You are a test assistant."
    return session
```

**Conversation history structure tested:**
```python
[
    {"role": "user", "content": "Find me a jacket"},
    {"role": "assistant", "content": [_make_tool_use_block()]},
    {"role": "user", "content": [{"type": "tool_result", "tool_use_id": "tu_123", "content": "{}"}]},
    {"role": "assistant", "content": [_make_text_block("Here's what I found")]},
]
```

#### **5. Socket.io Mocking**

**Simple emit test:**
```python
sio = AsyncMock()
await emit_scrape_progress(sio, user_id="user-123", purchases_count=5, ...)
sio.emit.assert_called_once_with("scrape_progress", {...}, room="user-123")
```

**Complex handler test (session_started):**
```python
with patch("main.get_neon_client", new_callable=AsyncMock) as mock_db, \
     patch("main.generate_outfit_recommendations", new_callable=AsyncMock, return_value=mock_result), \
     patch("main.sio", mock_sio):
    await session_started("sid-123", {"session_id": "sess-1", "user_id": "user-1"})
    
tool_result_calls = [c for c in mock_sio.emit.call_args_list if c.args[0] == "tool_result"]
assert payload["type"] == "display_product"
```

#### **6. Tool Execution Mocking**

**Using patch with side_effect:**
```python
with patch("scraper.pipeline.build_gmail_service", return_value=mock_svc):
    result = await fast_scrape(token_data={"access_token": "test"})
```

**Using AsyncMock for async tools:**
```python
with patch("agent.tools.fetch_clothing_batch", new_callable=AsyncMock, return_value=mock_items):
    result = await execute_give_recommendation({...}, session_id)
```

#### **7. Database Testing (Test Utilities)**

**NeonSQL helper class in test_database.py:**
```python
class NeonSQL:
    async def execute(self, query: str, params: list | None = None) -> list[dict]
    async def fetchval(self, query: str, params: list | None = None)
    async def fetchrow(self, query: str, params: list | None = None) -> dict | None
```

**Fixture pattern:**
```python
@pytest_asyncio.fixture(scope="session")
async def db():
    return NeonSQL(DATABASE_URL)

@pytest_asyncio.fixture
async def test_user(db):
    email = f"test_{uuid.uuid4().hex[:8]}@mirrorless.test"
    rows = await db.execute("INSERT INTO users ...")
    yield user_id
    await db.execute("DELETE FROM users ...")
```

#### **8. Error Recovery Testing**

**Testing conversation history cleanup on API failure:**
```python
@pytest.mark.asyncio
async def test_api_failure_pops_orphaned_tool_use_assistant():
    orch = _make_orchestrator()
    session = _make_session([original_user_msg, assistant_with_tool_use, user_tool_results])
    
    with patch.object(orch.client.messages, "stream", side_effect=Exception("API down")):
        await orch._call_claude(session)
    
    assert len(session.conversation_history) == 1  # Only original user msg remains
```

#### **9. Concurrency Testing**

**Simulating concurrent events:**
```python
await asyncio.gather(
    orch.handle_event("test-user", {"type": "voice", "transcript": "First"}),
    orch.handle_event("test-user", {"type": "voice", "transcript": "Second"}),
)

# Verify lock serialization prevents history corruption
assert session.conversation_history[0]["role"] == "user"
assert session.conversation_history[1]["role"] == "assistant"
assert session.conversation_history[2]["role"] == "user"
assert session.conversation_history[3]["role"] == "assistant"
```

#### **10. Integration Test Markers**

**Skip tests when API keys missing:**
```python
@pytest.mark.skipif(
    not os.getenv("SERPER_API_KEY") or not os.getenv("ANTHROPIC_API_KEY"),
    reason="Requires SERPER_API_KEY and ANTHROPIC_API_KEY",
)
async def test_generate_recommendations_integration(db, test_user_with_purchases, test_session):
    result = await generate_outfit_recommendations(test_user_with_purchases, test_session, db)
    assert result["status"] == "success"
```

### **Summary of Best Practices Found**

1. **AsyncMock for Socket.io** — Never sync mocks for async operations
2. **SimpleNamespace for Anthropic SDK blocks** — Mimics real SDK objects
3. **patch.object() for orchestrator methods** — Direct mocking of instance methods
4. **@pytest.mark.asyncio** — All async tests decorated
5. **Helper functions (_make_*) over inline mocks** — Reusable test object factories
6. **Session-scoped fixtures for DB** — Reduces test overhead
7. **Manual cleanup in yield fixtures** — Consistent test isolation
8. **Direct integration tests with live APIs** — Separate from unit tests with skipif markers
9. **Conversation history validation helpers** — Test utilities prevent history corruption

### **Absolute File Paths**
- `/Users/johnathanmo/mirrorless/backend/tests/test_mira.py`
- `/Users/johnathanmo/mirrorless/backend/tests/test_error_recovery.py`
- `/Users/johnathanmo/mirrorless/backend/tests/test_history_integrity.py`
- `/Users/johnathanmo/mirrorless/backend/tests/test_unified_pipeline.py`
- `/Users/johnathanmo/mirrorless/backend/tests/test_pipeline.py`
- `/Users/johnathanmo/mirrorless/backend/tests/test_socket_events.py`
- `/Users/johnathanmo/mirrorless/backend/tests/test_database.py`
- `/Users/johnathanmo/mirrorless/backend/tests/test_queue.py`

```python
async def test_generate_recommendations_integration(db, test_user_with_purchases, test_session):
    result = await generate_outfit_recommendations(test_user_with_purchases, test_session, db)
    assert result["status"] == "success"
```

### **Summary of Best Practices Found**

1. **AsyncMock for Socket.io** — Never sync mocks for async operations
2. **SimpleNamespace for Anthropic SDK blocks** — Mimics real SDK objects
3. **patch.object() for orchestrator methods** — Direct mocking of instance methods
4. **@pytest.mark.asyncio** — All async tests decorated
5. **Helper functions (_make_*) over inline mocks** — Reusable test object factories
6. **Session-scoped fixtures for DB** — Reduces test overhead
7. **Manual cleanup in yield fixtures** — Consistent test isolation
8. **Direct integration tests with live APIs** — Separate from unit tests with skipif markers
9. **Conversation history validation helpers** — Test utilities prevent history corruption

"""Tests for _call_claude error recovery — orphaned tool_use cleanup.

When a Claude API call fails mid-conversation, the error handler must clean up
the conversation history so subsequent calls don't hit permanent 400 errors from
unmatched tool_use/tool_result pairs.
"""

from types import SimpleNamespace
from unittest.mock import AsyncMock, MagicMock, patch

import pytest

from agent.orchestrator import MiraOrchestrator, SessionState


def _make_orchestrator() -> MiraOrchestrator:
    """Create an orchestrator with mocked Socket.io."""
    orch = MiraOrchestrator(socket_io=AsyncMock())
    return orch


def _make_session(history: list) -> SessionState:
    """Create a session with pre-populated conversation history."""
    session = SessionState(user_id="test-user")
    session.conversation_history = list(history)
    session.system_prompt = "You are a test assistant."
    return session


def _make_tool_use_block(**kwargs):
    """Create a SimpleNamespace mimicking an Anthropic SDK tool_use content block."""
    defaults = {"type": "tool_use", "id": "tu_123", "name": "search_clothing", "input": {}}
    defaults.update(kwargs)
    return SimpleNamespace(**defaults)


def _make_text_block(text="Hello"):
    """Create a SimpleNamespace mimicking an Anthropic SDK text content block."""
    return SimpleNamespace(type="text", text=text)


@pytest.mark.asyncio
async def test_api_failure_pops_user_message():
    """When the API fails, the last user message should be popped."""
    orch = _make_orchestrator()
    user_msg = {"role": "user", "content": "What should I wear?"}
    session = _make_session([user_msg])

    with patch.object(orch.client.messages, "stream", side_effect=Exception("API down")):
        await orch._call_claude(session)

    assert session.conversation_history == []


@pytest.mark.asyncio
async def test_api_failure_pops_orphaned_tool_use_assistant():
    """When the API fails after a tool cycle, both the user tool_results AND
    the assistant tool_use message should be popped to prevent 400 errors."""
    orch = _make_orchestrator()

    original_user_msg = {"role": "user", "content": "Find me a jacket"}
    assistant_with_tool_use = {
        "role": "assistant",
        "content": [_make_tool_use_block()],
    }
    user_tool_results = {
        "role": "user",
        "content": [{"type": "tool_result", "tool_use_id": "tu_123", "content": "{}"}],
    }
    session = _make_session([original_user_msg, assistant_with_tool_use, user_tool_results])

    with patch.object(orch.client.messages, "stream", side_effect=Exception("API down")):
        await orch._call_claude(session)

    # Only the original user message should remain
    assert len(session.conversation_history) == 1
    assert session.conversation_history[0] is original_user_msg


@pytest.mark.asyncio
async def test_api_failure_preserves_text_only_assistant():
    """When the API fails, a text-only assistant message should NOT be popped —
    only assistant messages containing tool_use blocks are orphaned."""
    orch = _make_orchestrator()

    user_msg = {"role": "user", "content": "Hello"}
    assistant_text_only = {
        "role": "assistant",
        "content": [_make_text_block("Hey there!")],
    }
    user_msg2 = {"role": "user", "content": "What's in style?"}
    session = _make_session([user_msg, assistant_text_only, user_msg2])

    with patch.object(orch.client.messages, "stream", side_effect=Exception("API down")):
        await orch._call_claude(session)

    # user_msg2 popped, but assistant_text_only preserved (no tool_use blocks)
    assert len(session.conversation_history) == 2
    assert session.conversation_history[0] is user_msg
    assert session.conversation_history[1] is assistant_text_only


@pytest.mark.asyncio
async def test_fallback_message_streamed_on_failure():
    """When the API fails, a fallback voice message should be streamed
    so the user gets verbal feedback."""
    orch = _make_orchestrator()
    session = _make_session([{"role": "user", "content": "Hi"}])

    with patch.object(orch.client.messages, "stream", side_effect=Exception("API down")):
        with patch.object(orch, "_stream_text", new_callable=AsyncMock) as mock_stream:
            await orch._call_claude(session)

    # Should have been called twice: once with fallback text, once with end_of_message
    assert mock_stream.call_count == 2
    fallback_call = mock_stream.call_args_list[0]
    assert "glitched" in fallback_call.args[1]
    eom_call = mock_stream.call_args_list[1]
    assert eom_call.args[1] == ""
    assert eom_call.kwargs.get("end_of_message") is True


@pytest.mark.asyncio
async def test_api_failure_handles_dict_tool_use_blocks():
    """The orphaned tool_use check should work with plain dict blocks
    (not just Anthropic SDK objects), since content can be either format."""
    orch = _make_orchestrator()

    original_user_msg = {"role": "user", "content": "Find me shoes"}
    assistant_with_dict_tool_use = {
        "role": "assistant",
        "content": [{"type": "tool_use", "id": "tu_456", "name": "search_clothing", "input": {}}],
    }
    user_tool_results = {
        "role": "user",
        "content": [{"type": "tool_result", "tool_use_id": "tu_456", "content": "{}"}],
    }
    session = _make_session([original_user_msg, assistant_with_dict_tool_use, user_tool_results])

    with patch.object(orch.client.messages, "stream", side_effect=Exception("API down")):
        await orch._call_claude(session)

    # Both tool_results user msg and tool_use assistant msg should be popped
    assert len(session.conversation_history) == 1
    assert session.conversation_history[0] is original_user_msg

"""Tests for TTS deduplication — fire-and-forget tool loop prevention + model selection.

Validates three fixes that prevent TTS audio cutoff:
1. _handle_tool_calls skips re-calling Claude after fire-and-forget tools
2. _select_model always returns Sonnet with 2048 max_tokens
3. Mixed tool batches (fire-and-forget + data-returning) still re-call Claude
"""

from types import SimpleNamespace
from unittest.mock import AsyncMock, patch

import pytest

from agent.orchestrator import MiraOrchestrator, SessionState, SONNET_MODEL


def _make_orchestrator() -> MiraOrchestrator:
    """Create an orchestrator with mocked Socket.io."""
    return MiraOrchestrator(socket_io=AsyncMock())


def _make_session(history: list | None = None) -> SessionState:
    """Create a session with pre-populated conversation history."""
    session = SessionState(user_id="test-user")
    session.conversation_history = list(history) if history else []
    session.system_prompt = "You are a test assistant."
    return session


def _make_tool_use_block(**kwargs):
    """Create a SimpleNamespace mimicking an Anthropic SDK tool_use content block."""
    defaults = {"type": "tool_use", "id": "tu_001", "name": "search_clothing", "input": {}}
    defaults.update(kwargs)
    return SimpleNamespace(**defaults)


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Change 1: Fire-and-forget tools skip re-calling Claude
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------


@pytest.mark.asyncio
async def test_send_voice_skips_claude_recall():
    """send_voice_to_client is fire-and-forget — should NOT re-call Claude."""
    orch = _make_orchestrator()
    session = _make_session([
        {"role": "user", "content": "Hello"},
        {
            "role": "assistant",
            "content": [
                SimpleNamespace(type="text", text="Hey there!"),
                _make_tool_use_block(id="tu_voice", name="send_voice_to_client", input={"text": "Hey there!"}),
            ],
        },
    ])

    tool_uses = [_make_tool_use_block(id="tu_voice", name="send_voice_to_client", input={"text": "Hey there!"})]

    with patch("agent.orchestrator.execute_tool", new_callable=AsyncMock, return_value={"sent": True}):
        with patch.object(orch, "_call_claude", new_callable=AsyncMock) as mock_call:
            await orch._handle_tool_calls(session, tool_uses, tool_depth=0)

    mock_call.assert_not_called()


@pytest.mark.asyncio
async def test_data_returning_tool_triggers_claude_recall():
    """Tools like display_product return data Claude needs — should re-call Claude."""
    orch = _make_orchestrator()
    session = _make_session([
        {"role": "user", "content": "Show me jackets"},
        {
            "role": "assistant",
            "content": [_make_tool_use_block(id="tu_display", name="display_product")],
        },
    ])

    tool_uses = [_make_tool_use_block(id="tu_display", name="display_product", input={"items": []})]
    mock_result = {"items": [{"title": "Jacket", "price": "$50"}], "frontend_payload": {"type": "display_product", "items": []}}

    with patch("agent.orchestrator.execute_tool", new_callable=AsyncMock, return_value=mock_result):
        with patch.object(orch, "_call_claude", new_callable=AsyncMock) as mock_call:
            await orch._handle_tool_calls(session, tool_uses, tool_depth=0)

    mock_call.assert_called_once_with(session, tool_depth=1)


@pytest.mark.asyncio
async def test_mixed_tools_still_recall_claude():
    """When fire-and-forget AND data-returning tools are called together,
    Claude must still be re-called (the data tool needs processing)."""
    orch = _make_orchestrator()
    session = _make_session([
        {"role": "user", "content": "Show me something"},
        {
            "role": "assistant",
            "content": [
                _make_tool_use_block(id="tu_voice", name="send_voice_to_client"),
                _make_tool_use_block(id="tu_display", name="display_product"),
            ],
        },
    ])

    tool_uses = [
        _make_tool_use_block(id="tu_voice", name="send_voice_to_client", input={"text": "Check this out"}),
        _make_tool_use_block(id="tu_display", name="display_product", input={"items": []}),
    ]

    async def mock_execute(tool_name, tool_input, user_context):
        if tool_name == "send_voice_to_client":
            return {"sent": True}
        return {"items": [], "frontend_payload": {"type": "display_product", "items": []}}

    with patch("agent.orchestrator.execute_tool", new_callable=AsyncMock, side_effect=mock_execute):
        with patch.object(orch, "_call_claude", new_callable=AsyncMock) as mock_call:
            await orch._handle_tool_calls(session, tool_uses, tool_depth=0)

    mock_call.assert_called_once_with(session, tool_depth=1)


@pytest.mark.asyncio
async def test_tool_results_appended_even_when_skipping_recall():
    """Even when skipping the Claude re-call, tool results must still be
    appended to conversation history (keeps the tool_use/tool_result pairing valid)."""
    orch = _make_orchestrator()
    session = _make_session([
        {"role": "user", "content": "Hi"},
        {
            "role": "assistant",
            "content": [_make_tool_use_block(id="tu_voice", name="send_voice_to_client")],
        },
    ])

    tool_uses = [_make_tool_use_block(id="tu_voice", name="send_voice_to_client", input={"text": "Hi!"})]

    with patch("agent.orchestrator.execute_tool", new_callable=AsyncMock, return_value={"sent": True}):
        with patch.object(orch, "_call_claude", new_callable=AsyncMock):
            await orch._handle_tool_calls(session, tool_uses, tool_depth=0)

    # Tool result message should be the last entry in history
    last_msg = session.conversation_history[-1]
    assert last_msg["role"] == "user"
    assert isinstance(last_msg["content"], list)
    assert last_msg["content"][0]["type"] == "tool_result"
    assert last_msg["content"][0]["tool_use_id"] == "tu_voice"


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Change 2: _select_model always returns Sonnet with 2048 max_tokens
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------


def test_select_model_returns_sonnet_for_conversational_turn():
    """Conversational turns should use Sonnet with 2048 tokens."""
    orch = _make_orchestrator()
    session = _make_session([{"role": "user", "content": "What should I wear?"}])

    model, max_tokens = orch._select_model(session)

    assert model == SONNET_MODEL
    assert max_tokens == 2048


def test_select_model_returns_sonnet_for_tool_result_turn():
    """Tool-result turns should ALSO use Sonnet (no more Haiku downgrade)."""
    orch = _make_orchestrator()
    session = _make_session([
        {"role": "user", "content": "Show me jackets"},
        {
            "role": "assistant",
            "content": [_make_tool_use_block()],
        },
        {
            "role": "user",
            "content": [{"type": "tool_result", "tool_use_id": "tu_001", "content": "{}"}],
        },
    ])

    model, max_tokens = orch._select_model(session)

    assert model == SONNET_MODEL
    assert max_tokens == 2048


def test_select_model_returns_sonnet_for_empty_history():
    """Empty history (session start) should use Sonnet."""
    orch = _make_orchestrator()
    session = _make_session([])

    model, max_tokens = orch._select_model(session)

    assert model == SONNET_MODEL
    assert max_tokens == 2048


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Change 3: Frontend payload emission still works for non-voice tools
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------


@pytest.mark.asyncio
async def test_frontend_payload_emitted_for_display_product():
    """display_product frontend_payload should still be emitted via Socket.io
    (only voice_message was removed from the frontend handler)."""
    orch = _make_orchestrator()
    session = _make_session([
        {"role": "user", "content": "Show me outfits"},
        {
            "role": "assistant",
            "content": [_make_tool_use_block(id="tu_dp", name="display_product")],
        },
    ])

    tool_uses = [_make_tool_use_block(id="tu_dp", name="display_product", input={"items": []})]
    payload = {"type": "display_product", "items": [{"title": "Jacket"}]}
    mock_result = {"items": [{"title": "Jacket"}], "frontend_payload": payload}

    with patch("agent.orchestrator.execute_tool", new_callable=AsyncMock, return_value=mock_result):
        with patch.object(orch, "_call_claude", new_callable=AsyncMock):
            await orch._handle_tool_calls(session, tool_uses, tool_depth=0)

    orch.sio.emit.assert_called_once_with("tool_result", payload, room="test-user")


@pytest.mark.asyncio
async def test_voice_tool_frontend_payload_still_emitted():
    """send_voice_to_client may still emit a frontend_payload — the backend
    doesn't filter it. The dedup fix is on the frontend side (mirror/page.tsx
    ignores voice_message type)."""
    orch = _make_orchestrator()
    session = _make_session([
        {"role": "user", "content": "Hi"},
        {
            "role": "assistant",
            "content": [_make_tool_use_block(id="tu_v", name="send_voice_to_client")],
        },
    ])

    tool_uses = [_make_tool_use_block(id="tu_v", name="send_voice_to_client", input={"text": "Hey!"})]
    payload = {"type": "voice_message", "text": "Hey!"}
    mock_result = {"sent": True, "frontend_payload": payload}

    with patch("agent.orchestrator.execute_tool", new_callable=AsyncMock, return_value=mock_result):
        with patch.object(orch, "_call_claude", new_callable=AsyncMock):
            await orch._handle_tool_calls(session, tool_uses, tool_depth=0)

    # Backend still emits the payload — frontend just ignores voice_message type now
    orch.sio.emit.assert_called_once_with("tool_result", payload, room="test-user")

"""Tests for TTS deduplication — fire-and-forget tool loop prevention + model selection.

Validates three fixes that prevent TTS audio cutoff:
1. _handle_tool_calls skips re-calling Claude after fire-and-forget tools
2. _select_model always returns Sonnet with 2048 max_tokens
3. Mixed tool batches (fire-and-forget + data-returning) still re-call Claude
"""

from types import SimpleNamespace
from unittest.mock import AsyncMock, patch

import pytest

from agent.orchestrator import MiraOrchestrator, SessionState, SONNET_MODEL


def _make_orchestrator() -> MiraOrchestrator:
    """Create an orchestrator with mocked Socket.io."""
    return MiraOrchestrator(socket_io=AsyncMock())


def _make_session(history: list | None = None) -> SessionState:
    """Create a session with pre-populated conversation history."""
    session = SessionState(user_id="test-user")
    session.conversation_history = list(history) if history else []
    session.system_prompt = "You are a test assistant."
    return session


def _make_tool_use_block(**kwargs):
    """Create a SimpleNamespace mimicking an Anthropic SDK tool_use content block."""
    defaults = {"type": "tool_use", "id": "tu_001", "name": "search_clothing", "input": {}}
    defaults.update(kwargs)
    return SimpleNamespace(**defaults)


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Change 1: Fire-and-forget tools skip re-calling Claude
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------


@pytest.mark.asyncio
async def test_send_voice_skips_claude_recall():
    """send_voice_to_client is fire-and-forget — should NOT re-call Claude."""
    orch = _make_orchestrator()
    session = _make_session([
        {"role": "user", "content": "Hello"},
        {
            "role": "assistant",
            "content": [
                SimpleNamespace(type="text", text="Hey there!"),
                _make_tool_use_block(id="tu_voice", name="send_voice_to_client", input={"text": "Hey there!"}),
            ],
        },
    ])

    tool_uses = [_make_tool_use_block(id="tu_voice", name="send_voice_to_client", input={"text": "Hey there!"})]

    with patch("agent.orchestrator.execute_tool", new_callable=AsyncMock, return_value={"sent": True}):
        with patch.object(orch, "_call_claude", new_callable=AsyncMock) as mock_call:
            await orch._handle_tool_calls(session, tool_uses, tool_depth=0)

    mock_call.assert_not_called()


@pytest.mark.asyncio
async def test_data_returning_tool_triggers_claude_recall():
    """Tools like display_product return data Claude needs — should re-call Claude."""
    orch = _make_orchestrator()
    session = _make_session([
        {"role": "user", "content": "Show me jackets"},
        {
            "role": "assistant",
            "content": [_make_tool_use_block(id="tu_display", name="display_product")],
        },
    ])

    tool_uses = [_make_tool_use_block(id="tu_display", name="display_product", input={"items": []})]
    mock_result = {"items": [{"title": "Jacket", "price": "$50"}], "frontend_payload": {"type": "display_product", "items": []}}

    with patch("agent.orchestrator.execute_tool", new_callable=AsyncMock, return_value=mock_result):
        with patch.object(orch, "_call_claude", new_callable=AsyncMock) as mock_call:
            await orch._handle_tool_calls(session, tool_uses, tool_depth=0)

    mock_call.assert_called_once_with(session, tool_depth=1)


@pytest.mark.asyncio
async def test_mixed_tools_still_recall_claude():
    """When fire-and-forget AND data-returning tools are called together,
    Claude must still be re-called (the data tool needs processing)."""
    orch = _make_orchestrator()
    session = _make_session([
        {"role": "user", "content": "Show me something"},
        {
            "role": "assistant",
            "content": [
                _make_tool_use_block(id="tu_voice", name="send_voice_to_client"),
                _make_tool_use_block(id="tu_display", name="display_product"),
            ],
        },
    ])

    tool_uses = [
        _make_tool_use_block(id="tu_voice", name="send_voice_to_client", input={"text": "Check this out"}),
        _make_tool_use_block(id="tu_display", name="display_product", input={"items": []}),
    ]

    async def mock_execute(tool_name, tool_input, user_context):
        if tool_name == "send_voice_to_client":
            return {"sent": True}
        return {"items": [], "frontend_payload": {"type": "display_product", "items": []}}

    with patch("agent.orchestrator.execute_tool", new_callable=AsyncMock, side_effect=mock_execute):
        with patch.object(orch, "_call_claude", new_callable=AsyncMock) as mock_call:
            await orch._handle_tool_calls(session, tool_uses, tool_depth=0)

    mock_call.assert_called_once_with(session, tool_depth=1)


@pytest.mark.asyncio
async def test_tool_results_appended_even_when_skipping_recall():
    """Even when skipping the Claude re-call, tool results must still be
    appended to conversation history (keeps the tool_use/tool_result pairing valid)."""
    orch = _make_orchestrator()
    session = _make_session([
        {"role": "user", "content": "Hi"},
        {
            "role": "assistant",
            "content": [_make_tool_use_block(id="tu_voice", name="send_voice_to_client")],
        },
    ])

    tool_uses = [_make_tool_use_block(id="tu_voice", name="send_voice_to_client", input={"text": "Hi!"})]

    with patch("agent.orchestrator.execute_tool", new_callable=AsyncMock, return_value={"sent": True}):
        with patch.object(orch, "_call_claude", new_callable=AsyncMock):
            await orch._handle_tool_calls(session, tool_uses, tool_depth=0)

    # Tool result message should be the last entry in history
    last_msg = session.conversation_history[-1]
    assert last_msg["role"] == "user"
    assert isinstance(last_msg["content"], list)
    assert last_msg["content"][0]["type"] == "tool_result"
    assert last_msg["content"][0]["tool_use_id"] == "tu_voice"


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Change 2: _select_model always returns Sonnet with 2048 max_tokens
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------


def test_select_model_returns_sonnet_for_conversational_turn():
    """Conversational turns should use Sonnet with 2048 tokens."""
    orch = _make_orchestrator()
    session = _make_session([{"role": "user", "content": "What should I wear?"}])

    model, max_tokens = orch._select_model(session)

    assert model == SONNET_MODEL
    assert max_tokens == 2048


def test_select_model_returns_sonnet_for_tool_result_turn():
    """Tool-result turns should ALSO use Sonnet (no more Haiku downgrade)."""
    orch = _make_orchestrator()
    session = _make_session([
        {"role": "user", "content": "Show me jackets"},
        {
            "role": "assistant",
            "content": [_make_tool_use_block()],
        },
        {
            "role": "user",
            "content": [{"type": "tool_result", "tool_use_id": "tu_001", "content": "{}"}],
        },
    ])

    model, max_tokens = orch._select_model(session)

    assert model == SONNET_MODEL
    assert max_tokens == 2048


def test_select_model_returns_sonnet_for_empty_history():
    """Empty history (session start) should use Sonnet."""
    orch = _make_orchestrator()
    session = _make_session([])

    model, max_tokens = orch._select_model(session)

    assert model == SONNET_MODEL
    assert max_tokens == 2048


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Change 3: Frontend payload emission still works for non-voice tools
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------


@pytest.mark.asyncio
async def test_frontend_payload_emitted_for_display_product():
    """display_product frontend_payload should still be emitted via Socket.io
    (only voice_message was removed from the frontend handler)."""
    orch = _make_orchestrator()
    session = _make_session([
        {"role": "user", "content": "Show me outfits"},
        {
            "role": "assistant",
            "content": [_make_tool_use_block(id="tu_dp", name="display_product")],
        },
    ])

    tool_uses = [_make_tool_use_block(id="tu_dp", name="display_product", input={"items": []})]
    payload = {"type": "display_product", "items": [{"title": "Jacket"}]}
    mock_result = {"items": [{"title": "Jacket"}], "frontend_payload": payload}

    with patch("agent.orchestrator.execute_tool", new_callable=AsyncMock, return_value=mock_result):
        with patch.object(orch, "_call_claude", new_callable=AsyncMock):
            await orch._handle_tool_calls(session, tool_uses, tool_depth=0)

    orch.sio.emit.assert_called_once_with("tool_result", payload, room="test-user")


@pytest.mark.asyncio
async def test_voice_tool_frontend_payload_still_emitted():
    """send_voice_to_client may still emit a frontend_payload — the backend
    doesn't filter it. The dedup fix is on the frontend side (mirror/page.tsx
    ignores voice_message type)."""
    orch = _make_orchestrator()
    session = _make_session([
        {"role": "user", "content": "Hi"},
        {
            "role": "assistant",
            "content": [_make_tool_use_block(id="tu_v", name="send_voice_to_client")],
        },
    ])

    tool_uses = [_make_tool_use_block(id="tu_v", name="send_voice_to_client", input={"text": "Hey!"})]
    payload = {"type": "voice_message", "text": "Hey!"}
    mock_result = {"sent": True, "frontend_payload": payload}

    with patch("agent.orchestrator.execute_tool", new_callable=AsyncMock, return_value=mock_result):
        with patch.object(orch, "_call_claude", new_callable=AsyncMock):
            await orch._handle_tool_calls(session, tool_uses, tool_depth=0)

    # Backend still emits the payload — frontend just ignores voice_message type now
    orch.sio.emit.assert_called_once_with("tool_result", payload, room="test-user")

All 9 tests pass.

`★ Insight ─────────────────────────────────────`
The tests cover three distinct layers of the fix:

1. **Fire-and-forget loop prevention** (4 tests): The critical `issubset()` logic is tested with three scenarios — pure fire-and-forget (skip), pure data-returning (recall), and mixed (recall). A fourth test verifies that tool results are still appended to conversation history even when the recall is skipped, which is essential for maintaining valid tool_use/tool_result pairing in the Anthropic API.

2. **Model selection simplification** (3 tests): These verify the Haiku downgrade is gone. The tool-result turn test is the key one — it would have returned `HAIKU_MODEL` before our fix. Testing empty history covers the session-start edge case.

3. **Socket.io emission integrity** (2 tests): These confirm that removing the frontend `voice_message` handler doesn't break the backend's emission path. The backend still emits `voice_message` payloads — the dedup is purely on the frontend side (the mirror page now ignores them).
`─────────────────────────────────────────────────`

# Plan: Fix TTS audio getting cut off — two root causes

## Context

After applying the snapshot delay fix, TTS still gets cut off. Investigation of the backend logs reveals **two additional root causes** that compound to create the problem:

1. **Duplicate TTS requests**: Every Mira response triggers TTS **twice** — once via `mira_speech` streaming, once via `send_voice_to_client` tool result
2. **Agent turn looping**: After calling `send_voice_to_client`, the orchestrator unconditionally calls Claude again, which generates a near-identical response (and another `send_voice_to_client`), creating a feedback loop

The combined effect: one user message → 4+ TTS requests hitting the frontend in rapid succession, each killing the previous via the generation counter.

---

## Root Cause 1: Duplicate TTS per message

**Two parallel paths trigger `mira.speak()` on the frontend for the same text:**

**Path A — Streaming chunks** (`mira_speech` socket events):
- `orchestrator.py:398-402` streams each Claude text delta via `mira_speech`
- `mirror/page.tsx:188-224` accumulates chunks, calls `mira.speak(cleanText)` on end-of-message

**Path B — Tool result** (`tool_result` socket event):
- Claude also calls `send_voice_to_client` tool with the same text
- `tools.py:510-518` returns a `frontend_payload` with `type: "voice_message"`
- `orchestrator.py:480-486` emits this as a `tool_result` socket event
- `mirror/page.tsx:263-266` catches `voice_message` type and calls `mira.speak()` again

Both paths fire for the same content → two `/api/tts/stream` requests → second one kills the first via generation counter.

## Root Cause 2: Agent turn looping

**`_handle_tool_calls()` always re-calls Claude after processing tool results:**

- `orchestrator.py:509-510`: After executing tools, it unconditionally calls `await self._call_claude(session, tool_depth=tool_depth + 1)`
- When `send_voice_to_client` returns `{"sent": True}`, Claude receives this as a tool result and generates another near-identical spoken response
- This creates: turn #5 (sonnet, response) → turn #6 (haiku, processes tool result) → turn #7 (sonnet, near-duplicate response) → turn #8 (haiku) → ...

The haiku/sonnet alternation comes from `_select_model()` (line 264-280): tool-result turns use Haiku, conversational turns use Sonnet.

---

## Fix

### Change 1: Stop the agent loop after `send_voice_to_client`

**File**: `backend/agent/orchestrator.py` — `_handle_tool_calls()` method (line ~503-510)

After collecting tool results, check if **all** tool calls were fire-and-forget (i.e., `send_voice_to_client`). If so, skip the re-call to Claude. Only re-call Claude when there are tools that return meaningful data Claude needs to process (like `give_recommendation`, `search_purchases`, etc.).

```python
# Add tool results to conversation and continue
session.conversation_history.append({
    "role": "user",
    "content": tool_results,
})

# Only call Claude again if there are tools that return data Claude needs to process.
# Fire-and-forget tools (like send_voice_to_client) don't need a follow-up turn.
FIRE_AND_FORGET_TOOLS = {"send_voice_to_client"}
tool_names = {tu.name for tu in tool_uses}
if not tool_names.issubset(FIRE_AND_FORGET_TOOLS):
    await self._call_claude(session, tool_depth=tool_depth + 1)
```

This stops the loop: Claude says something + calls `send_voice_to_client` → tool executes → **done** (no re-call).

### Change 2: Use Sonnet for all agent turns (remove Haiku downgrade)

**File**: `backend/agent/orchestrator.py` — `_select_model()` method (line ~264-280)

Remove the Haiku branching logic entirely. Always use Sonnet with generous token limits:

```python
def _select_model(self, session: SessionState) -> tuple[str, int]:
    """Select the right model and max_tokens for this turn."""
    return SONNET_MODEL, 2048
```

- **Always Sonnet** — no more Haiku for any turn (conversational or tool-result processing)
- **Always 2048 max_tokens** — no more restricting conversational turns to 400 tokens, giving Mira room to be expressive
- This eliminates the model alternation entirely and gives the best conversational experience across all turns

### Change 3: Remove the duplicate `voice_message` TTS path on the frontend

**File**: `frontend/src/app/mirror/page.tsx` — `handleToolResult` (line ~263-266)

Remove the `voice_message` branch that calls `mira.speak()`, since the streaming `mira_speech` path already handles TTS for all Claude responses:

```typescript
// REMOVE this block:
if (data.type === "voice_message" && data.text) {
    const emotion = detectEmotionFromText(data.text);
    mira.speak(data.text, emotion);
    return;
}
```

The `mira_speech` streaming path (Path A) is the primary pipeline — it includes emotion tag parsing, chunk accumulation, and proper emotion detection. The `voice_message` path (Path B) was a redundant second trigger.

---

## Files Modified

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `backend/agent/orchestrator.py` | Skip re-calling Claude after fire-and-forget tools; use Sonnet for all turns |
| `frontend/src/app/mirror/page.tsx` | Remove `voice_message` → `mira.speak()` duplicate path |

## Verification

1. Start a mirror session
2. Check backend logs: each user message should produce **one** Claude response (not 2+), and **one** `[tts] TTS stream request` per message (not 2+)
3. First greeting should play fully without cutoff
4. Normal conversation should work — each response plays once
5. Barge-in (speaking while Mira is talking) should still interrupt her
6. `display_product` tool results should still render clothing items on the canvas (this path is unchanged)
7. Tools that return data Claude needs (like `give_recommendation`) should still trigger a follow-up Claude turn

"""Admin endpoints: queue management, session info, booth stats."""

from fastapi import APIRouter, Request

from models.database import NeonHTTPClient

router = APIRouter(prefix="/admin", tags=["admin"])


@router.get("/queue")
async def get_admin_queue():
    """Full queue with user names and statuses."""
    db = NeonHTTPClient()
    try:
        rows = await db.execute(
            """
            SELECT q.id, q.user_id, u.name, q.position, q.status, q.joined_at
            FROM queue q
            JOIN users u ON u.id = q.user_id
            WHERE q.status IN ('waiting', 'active')
            ORDER BY q.position
            """,
        )
        return [
            {
                "id": str(r["id"]),
                "user_id": str(r["user_id"]),
                "name": r["name"],
                "position": r["position"],
                "status": r["status"],
                "joined_at": str(r["joined_at"]) if r.get("joined_at") else None,
            }
            for r in (rows or [])
        ]
    finally:
        await db.close()


@router.get("/session")
async def get_admin_session():
    """Current active session info."""
    db = NeonHTTPClient()
    try:
        # Find the active queue user
        active_rows = await db.execute(
            """
            SELECT q.user_id, u.name, u.email
            FROM queue q
            JOIN users u ON u.id = q.user_id
            WHERE q.status = 'active'
            LIMIT 1
            """,
        )
        if not active_rows:
            return None

        user_id = str(active_rows[0]["user_id"])

        # Find active session for this user
        session_rows = await db.execute(
            """
            SELECT id, api_call_count FROM sessions
            WHERE user_id = $1::uuid AND status = 'active'
            ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT 1
            """,
            [user_id],
        )

        session_id = None
        api_calls = 0
        if session_rows:
            session_id = str(session_rows[0]["id"])
            api_calls = session_rows[0].get("api_call_count", 0) or 0

        # Count items shown/liked for this session
        items_shown = 0
        items_liked = 0
        if session_id:
            shown_val = await db.fetchval(
                "SELECT COUNT(*) FROM outfit_items WHERE session_id = $1::uuid",
                [session_id],
            )
            items_shown = int(shown_val) if shown_val else 0

            liked_val = await db.fetchval(
                "SELECT COUNT(*) FROM outfit_items WHERE session_id = $1::uuid AND reaction = 'liked'",
                [session_id],
            )
            items_liked = int(liked_val) if liked_val else 0

        return {
            "user_id": user_id,
            "name": active_rows[0]["name"],
            "session_id": session_id,
            "api_calls": api_calls,
            "items_shown": items_shown,
            "items_liked": items_liked,
        }
    finally:
        await db.close()


@router.get("/stats")
async def get_booth_stats():
    """Booth stats: total users today, avg session length, items shown/liked."""
    db = NeonHTTPClient()
    try:
        total_today = await db.fetchval(
            """
            SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT user_id) FROM queue
            WHERE joined_at::date = CURRENT_DATE
            """,
        )

        avg_seconds = await db.fetchval(
            """
            SELECT COALESCE(
                AVG(EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (ended_at - created_at))), 0
            )
            FROM sessions
            WHERE created_at::date = CURRENT_DATE AND status = 'completed'
            """,
        )

        total_shown = await db.fetchval(
            """
            SELECT COUNT(*) FROM outfit_items oi
            JOIN sessions s ON s.id = oi.session_id
            WHERE s.created_at::date = CURRENT_DATE
            """,
        )

        total_liked = await db.fetchval(
            """
            SELECT COUNT(*) FROM outfit_items oi
            JOIN sessions s ON s.id = oi.session_id
            WHERE s.created_at::date = CURRENT_DATE AND oi.reaction = 'liked'
            """,
        )

        return {
            "total_users_today": int(total_today) if total_today else 0,
            "avg_session_seconds": float(avg_seconds) if avg_seconds else 0,
            "total_items_shown": int(total_shown) if total_shown else 0,
            "total_items_liked": int(total_liked) if total_liked else 0,
        }
    finally:
        await db.close()


@router.post("/force-end")
async def force_end_session(request: Request):
    """Force-end the current active session."""
    sio = request.app.state.sio
    db = NeonHTTPClient()
    try:
        active_rows = await db.execute(
            """
            SELECT q.user_id FROM queue q
            WHERE q.status = 'active' LIMIT 1
            """,
        )
        if not active_rows:
            return {"status": "no_active_session"}

        user_id = str(active_rows[0]["user_id"])
        await sio.emit("session_force_end", {"user_id": user_id}, room="mirror")
        await sio.emit("session_force_end", {"user_id": user_id}, room=user_id)

        return {"status": "force_ended", "user_id": user_id}
    finally:
        await db.close()

1→-- 001_initial_schema.sql
     2→-- Creates all tables for the Mirrorless application
     3→
     4→CREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS "pgcrypto";
     5→
     6→CREATE TABLE users (
     7→  id uuid PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
     8→  name text NOT NULL,
     9→  email text UNIQUE NOT NULL,
    10→  phone text,
    11→  google_oauth_token jsonb,
    12→  poke_id text,
    13→  created_at timestamptz NOT NULL DEFAULT now()
    14→);
    15→
    16→CREATE TABLE style_profiles (
    17→  user_id uuid PRIMARY KEY REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    18→  brands text[] DEFAULT '{}',
    19→  price_range jsonb,
    20→  style_tags text[] DEFAULT '{}',
    21→  size_info jsonb,
    22→  narrative_summary text
    23→);
    24→
    25→CREATE TABLE purchases (
    26→  id uuid PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    27→  user_id uuid NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    28→  brand text NOT NULL,
    29→  item_name text NOT NULL,
    30→  category text,
    31→  price numeric,
    32→  date date,
    33→  source_email_id text
    34→);
    35→
    36→CREATE TABLE sessions (
    37→  id uuid PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    38→  user_id uuid NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    39→  started_at timestamptz NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
    40→  ended_at timestamptz,
    41→  status text NOT NULL DEFAULT 'active' CHECK (status IN ('active', 'completed', 'abandoned'))
    42→);
    43→
    44→CREATE TABLE clothing_items (
    45→  id uuid PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    46→  name text NOT NULL,
    47→  brand text,
    48→  price numeric,
    49→  image_url text,
    50→  buy_url text,
    51→  category text,
